# confluent_switchover_endpoint Resource

[![General Availability](https://img.shields.io/badge/Lifecycle%20Stage-General%20Availability-%2345c6e8)](https://docs.confluent.io/cloud/current/api.html#section/Versioning/API-Lifecycle-Policy)

`confluent_switchover_endpoint` provides a SwitchoverEndpoint resource that enables creating, editing, and deleting SwitchoverEndpoints on Confluent Cloud.

-> **Note:** It is recommended to set `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` on production instances to prevent accidental switchover endpoint deletion. This setting rejects plans that would destroy or recreate the switchover endpoint, such as attempting to change uneditable attributes. Read more about it in the [Terraform docs](https://www.terraform.io/language/meta-arguments/lifecycle#prevent_destroy).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "confluent_switchover_endpoint" "example" {
  display_name = "prod-kafka-dr-endpoint"
  endpoints = ""
  environment {
    id = "env-00000"
  }
  switchover_pair {
    id = "sw-00000"
  }
  target = "west-platt"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


- `display_name` - (Required String) A human-readable name for the switchover endpoint.
- `endpoints` - (Required String) The endpoint definitions, one per side (e.g. west/east). Must contain exactly 2 entries.
- `environment` - (Required Configuration Block) The environment to which this belongs. Supports the following:
    - `id` - (Required String) The ID of the Environment.
- `switchover_pair` - (Required Configuration Block) The switchover pair this endpoint is bound to. Supports the following:
    - `id` - (Required String) The ID of the Switchover Pair.
- `target` - (Optional String) The name of the endpoint that should be active. For stateful pairs the control plane owns this value (it follows the pair's active member) and customers cannot set it directly; on create it may be provided as an initial value. Must match one of `endpoints[].name`.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the preceding arguments, the following attributes are exported:


- `dr_endpoint` - (Required String) The sticky global DR endpoint hostname that clients connect to. This is a server-computed, read-only attribute (the backend sets it and exposes it on the spec, consistent with how other Confluent resources expose computed endpoints, e.g. `cmk.v2.Cluster`). It follows the pattern `glkc-$switchoverPairId-$switchoverEndpointId.global.confluent.cloud`.
- `api_version` - (Required String) API Version defines the schema version of this representation of a resource.
- `kind` - (Required String) Kind defines the object this REST resource represents.
- `resource_name` - (Required String) The Confluent Resource Name of the resource.

## Import

-> **Note:** `CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY` and `CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET` environment variables must be set before importing a SwitchoverEndpoint.

You can import a SwitchoverEndpoint by using the composite ID `<Environment ID>/<SwitchoverEndpoint ID>`, for example:

```shell
$ export CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_KEY="<cloud_api_key>"
$ export CONFLUENT_CLOUD_API_SECRET="<cloud_api_secret>"
$ terraform import confluent_switchover_endpoint.example env-abc123/swi-abc123
```

!> **Warning:** Do not forget to delete terminal command history afterwards for security purposes.
